response.justification: The candidate is an experienced full-stack/web developer, but the role's hard requirements are largely unmet: there is no evidence of native-level Japanese proficiency, no Linux kernel investigation or modification experience, and only brief C/C++ exposure (a ~7-month stint at National Instruments) versus the required strong C-for-UNIX proficiency. Security technologies (MACsec, 802.1X, captive portal), L2/L3 networking, and switch/embedded firmware experience are absent from the resume.
strengths: Broad, long software engineering career spanning web, mobile, desktop, and some embedded/hardware-adjacent work
Strong testing and craftsmanship culture (TDD, CI/CD, test tooling authorship)
Some systems-level exposure: C/C++ at National Instruments, low-latency DLang services, Linux familiarity
concerns: No indication of Japanese language ability — a stated hard requirement
No Linux kernel, embedded networking, switch, or firmware development experience
No experience with the required security technologies (MACsec, 802.1X, MAC authentication, tamper detection)

key_factors.factor: No embedded networking/security or Linux kernel experience
Solid general engineering experience and testing discipline
